questions.transcribedStem: A mixed fruit juice is made using 600 ml of orange juice and 1 litre of apple juice. To make 4 litres of the mixed fruit juice, how many litres of orange juice will be needed?
questions.answer: Total volume of 600 ml of orange juice and 1 l of apple juice -> 600 ml + 1 l = 0.6 l + 1 l = 1.6 l | Number of sets of 1.6 l in 4 l -> 4 l \div 1.6 l = 2.5 | 2.5 \times 0.6 = 1.5 l | 1.5 l of orange juice will be needed.

questions.transcribedStem: A pen costs $$2 more than a ruler. The total cost of 4 such pens is $$y.
questions.answer: (a) Number of sets of 4 pens in 12 pens -> 12 \div 4 = 3 | 3 \times $$y = $$3y | The cost of 12 such pens is $$3y. | (b) Cost of a pen -> $$y \div 4 = $$y/4 | $$y/4 - $$2 = $$(y/4 - 2) | The cost of a ruler is $$(y/4 - 2).

questions.transcribedStem: The number of visitors to a museum was 9000 in August. This was a 20% increase from the number in July. How many visitors were there in July?
questions.answer: 100% + 20% = 120% | 120% -> 9000 visitors | 1% -> 9000 \div 120 = 75 visitors | 100% -> 100 \times 75 = 7500 visitors | There were 7500 visitors in July.

questions.transcribedStem: Four girls shared the cost of a cake equally. When calculating the amount for each share, one of the girls made a mistake by dividing the cost of the cake by 3 instead of 4. Each girl paid $$2.40 more than her share. What should be the correct amount for each share?
questions.answer: Each girl paid $$2.40 more than her share, which means the additional amount of money they paid altogether was for the share of the girl who did not pay. | 3 \times $$2.40 = $$7.20 | The correct amount for each share should be $$7.20.

questions.transcribedStem: A total of 18 light bulbs are set up at an equal distance apart along three sides AB, BC and CD of a rectangular platform. The figure shows part of the set-up. The breadth of the platform is 260 cm. What is the length of the platform?
questions.answer: Distance between two consecutive light bulbs -> 260 \div 4 = 65 cm | Number of light bulbs along the length of the platform -> 18 - 4 - 4 = 10 | Number of intervals along the length of the platform -> 10 - 1 = 9 | 9 \times 65 = 585 cm | The length of the platform is 585 cm.

questions.transcribedStem: Mr Yip asked some customers to choose their favourite colour for a T-shirt. The results are shown in the graph below.
questions.answer: (a) Number of customers who chose red -> 6 | Number of customers who chose blue -> 30 | Number of customers who chose green -> 18 | The ratio of the number of customers who chose red to the number who chose blue to the number who chose green is 6 : 30 : 18. | (b) 6 : 30 : 18 = 1 : 5 : 3 | Number of units of blue T-shirts more than red T-shirts ordered -> 5 - 1 = 4 | Total number of units -> 1 + 5 + 3 = 9 | 9 units -> 630 T-shirts | 1 unit -> 630 \div 9 = 70 T-shirts | 4 units -> 4 \times 70 = 280 T-shirts | 280 more blue than red T-shirts were ordered.

questions.transcribedStem: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram and DEFG is a rhombus. CDE is a straight line. ∠ABC = 51°, ∠GAD = 25° and ∠FGE = 35°.
questions.answer: (a) \angle CDA = \angle CBA (opposite angles of a parallelogram) = 51^\circ | \angle GEF = \angle FGE (base angles of an isosceles triangle) = 35^\circ | \angle GFE = 180^\circ - 35^\circ - 35^\circ (sum of angles in a triangle) = 110^\circ | \angle GDE = \angle GFE (opposite angles of a rhombus) = 110^\circ | \angle ADG = 180^\circ - 51^\circ - 110^\circ (angles on a straight line) = 19^\circ | (b) \angle AGD = 180^\circ - 25^\circ - 19^\circ (sum of angles in a triangle) = 136^\circ | \angle DGE = (180^\circ - 110^\circ) \div 2 (base angles of an isosceles triangle) = 35^\circ | \angle AGF = 360^\circ - 136^\circ - 35^\circ - 35^\circ (angles at a point) = 154^\circ

questions.transcribedStem: Claire was given a job to make 200 toys. She made 8 toys each day from Monday to Friday and 15 each day on Saturday and Sunday. Starting on a Friday, on which day of the week did Claire complete the job? [3marks]
questions.answer: Number of toys Claire made in the first week -> 8 + 15 + 15 = 38 | Number of toys she made from Monday to Sunday -> (5 \times 8) + (2 \times 15) = 40 + 30 = 70 | Number of toys she needed to make after the first week -> 200 - 38 = 162 | Number of weeks she took to make 162 toys -> 162 \div 70 = 2 R 22 | 22 = 8 + 8 + 6 | Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday | Day 1, Day 2, Day 3 | Claire completed the job on Wednesday of the week.

questions.transcribedStem:
Alice and Huda started jogging from the same point of a 5 km track round a park. They started at the same time but in opposite directions. Alice jogged past Huda after 2 km. Both of them did not change their speeds throughout.
Alice took 40 min to complete one round. What was Huda's jogging speed in m/min? [3marks]
questions.answer: 5 km = 5000 m | 2 km = 2000 m | Time Alice took to jog 5000 m -> 40 min | Time Alice took to jog 1000 m -> 40 \div 5 = 8 min | Time Alice took to jog 2000 m -> 2 \times 8 = 16 min | Distance Huda jogged when they passed each other -> 5000 - 2000 = 3000 m | 3000 \div 16 = 187.5 m/min | Huda's jogging speed was 187.5 m/min.

questions.transcribedStem: Rudi, Sam and Ted had 162 stickers altogether. Rudi gave $\frac{3}{10}$ of his stickers to Sam and $\frac{1}{4}$ of his stickers to Ted. After that, all three boys had the same number of stickers. Who had more stickers at first, Sam or Ted, and how many more? [3marks]
questions.answer: 3/10 = 6/20 | 1/4 = 5/20 | Fraction of stickers Rudi had left -> 1 - 6/20 - 5/20 = 9/20 | 27 units -> 162 stickers | 1 unit -> 162 \div 27 = 6 stickers | 3 units -> 3 \times 6 = 18 stickers | 4 units -> 4 \times 6 = 24 stickers | Number of stickers Sam had at first -> 18 | Number of stickers Ted had at first -> 24 | Ted had more stickers at first. | 24 - 18 = 6 | He had 6 more stickers.

questions.transcribedStem: Four teams of students washed cars for charity. They collected $$10 for washing a small car and $$15 for a big car. The table shows the number of cars washed by three of the teams.
questions.answer: (a) Amount of money team A collected -> (15 \times $$10) + (5 \times $$15) = $$150 + $$75 = $$225 | Amount of money team B collected -> (11 \times $$10) + (9 \times $$15) = $$110 + $$135 = $$245 | Amount of money team C collected -> (8 \times $$10) + (10 \times $$15) = $$80 + $$150 = $$230 | Team B collected the most money. | They collected $$245. | (b) Amount of money collected more from washing a big car than a small car -> $$15 - $$10 = $$5 | Number of big cars team D washed more than team A -> $$30 \div $$5 = 6 | 5 + 6 = 11 | Team D washed 11 big cars.

questions.transcribedStem:
The participants of a quiz are divided equally into two groups. In the first group, there are 20 more boys than girls. In the second group, there are 12 more girls than boys. 45% of all the participants are girls.

questions.answer: Percentage of participants who are boys -> 100% - 45% = 55% | Percentage of boys more than girls -> 55% - 45% = 10% | Number of boys more than girls -> 20 - 12 = 8 | 10% -> 8 participants | 1% -> 8 \div 10 = 0.8 participant | 55% -> 55 \times 0.8 = 44 participants | 44 participants are boys.

questions.transcribedStem: Suyin baked some pies. She gave $\frac{1}{5}$ of them to her relatives and 30 of them to her friends. She was left with $\frac{2}{3}$ of the pies. She packed these into 18 boxes. Some boxes contained 6 pies while the rest contained 12.
questions.answer: 1/5 = 3/15 | 2/3 = 10/15 | (a) 2 units -> 30 pies | 1 unit -> 30 \div 2 = 15 pies | 10 units -> 10 \times 15 = 150 pies | 150 pies were packed into the 18 boxes. Final answer: 150 | (b) Assume all the pies are packed into boxes of 6. | Total number of pies packed -> 18 \times 6 = 108 | Difference from the actual number of pies packed -> 150 - 108 = 42 | Number of pies in each box of 12 more than in each box of 6 -> 12 - 6 = 6 | Number of boxes that contained 12 pies -> 42 \div 6 = 7 | 18 - 7 = 11 | 11 boxes contained 6 pies.  Final answer: 11

questions.answer: (a) Base area of rectangular block Y -> 8 \times 10 = 80 cm² | Base area of rectangular block Z -> 15 \times 10 = 150 cm² | Base area of rectangular block Z greater than that of rectangular block Y -> 150 - 80 = 70 cm² | 1470 \div 70 = 21 cm | The height of each block is 21 cm. | (b) Base area of the box -> 6 \times 150 = 900 cm² | 900 cm² = 30 cm \times 30 cm | Length of each side of the base of the box -> 30 cm | Number of block Y along the length of the box -> 30 \div 8 = 3 R 6 cm \approx 3 | Number of block Y along the breadth of the box -> 30 \div 10 = 3 | 3 \times 3 = 9 | At most, 9 of block Y can be packed into such a box.

questions.transcribedStem: A caterer prepares chicken wings for some people attending a barbeque. The ratio of the number of adults to the number of children attending is 2 : 3. Among the children, the ratio of the number of girls to that of boys is 4 : 1. A total of 210 chicken wings are prepared so that each adult gets 4 chicken wings and each child gets 2.
questions.answer: (a) Fraction of the people who are children -> 3/5 | Fraction of the children who are boys -> 1/5 | 1/5 \times 3/5 = 3/25 | 3/25 of the people attending the barbeque are boys. | (b) Let 2 adults and 3 children be in 1 group. | Number of chicken wings prepared for each group -> (2 \times 4) + (3 \times 2) = 8 + 6 = 14 | Number of groups -> 210 \div 14 = 15 | 15 \times 3 = 45 | 45 children are attending the barbeque.

questions.transcribedStem: A square is made up of four triangles P, Q, R and S. The area of P is $\frac{1}{9}$ the area of the square while the area of Q is $\frac{1}{6}$ the area of the square.

questions.transcribedStem:

To buy the red bag, Lina would need $$2.50 more than what she had. So Lina bought the blue bag. After that, she had $$2 left.
questions.answer: (a) Difference in the percentage discount = 20% - 15% = 5% | 5% of the original price = $$2 + $$2.50 = $$4.50 | 1% of the original price = $$4.50 ÷ 5 = $$0.90 | 100 × $$0.90 = $$90 | The original price of the red bag was $$90. | (b) (100% - 20% =) 80% → 80 × $$0.90 = $$72 | $$72 + $$2 = $$74 | Lina had $$74 at first.

questions.transcribedStem:
At a walkathon, each participant completed either a 3-km route, a 5-km route or an 8-km route. $\frac{1}{4}$ of the participants completed the 3-km route, $\frac{9}{20}$ of the participants completed the 5-km route and the rest completed the 8-km route.

For every 1 km a participant walked, $$4 was donated to charity. A total of $$8208 was donated to charity.
questions.answer: (a) 1/4 = 5/20 | Fraction of the participants who completed the 8-km route = 1 - 5/20 - 9/20 = 6/20 | The ratio is 5 : 9 : 6. | (b) Assume that 5 participants completed the 3-km route, 9 participants completed the 5-km route and 6 participants completed the 8-km route in one group. | Total distance walked in one group = 5 × 3 km + 9 × 5 km + 6 × 8 km = 108 km | Total distance walked = $$8208 ÷ $$4 = 2052 km | Number of groups = 2052 km ÷ 108 km = 19 | Total number of participants in each group = 5 + 9 + 6 = 20 | 19 × 20 = 380 | The total number of participants at the walkathon is 380.

He painted all the faces of the block.
questions.answer: (a) 2 × (10 cm × 5 cm) + 2 × (10 cm × 4 cm) + 2 × (5 cm × 4 cm) = 220 cm² | The total painted area is 220 cm². | (b)(i) Number of cubes along the length with none of the faces painted = 10 - 2 = 8 | Number of cubes along the breadth with none of the faces painted = 5 - 2 = 3 | Number of cubes along the height with none of the faces painted = 4 - 2 = 2 | 8 × 3 × 2 = 48 | 48 cubes have none of the faces painted. | (b)(ii) Number of cubes along a 10-cm edge with 2 of the faces painted = 8 | Total number of cubes along the 10-cm edges with 2 of the faces painted = 4 × 8 = 32 | Number of cubes along a 5-cm edge with 2 of the faces painted = 3 | Total number of cubes along the 5-cm edges with 2 of the faces painted = 4 × 3 = 12 | Number of cubes along a 4-cm edge with 2 of the faces painted = 2 | Total number of cubes along the 4-cm edges with 2 of the faces painted = 4 × 2 = 8 | 32 + 12 + 8 = 52 | 52 cubes have 2 of the faces painted.

questions.transcribedStem:
Robots A and B moved from the start point to the end point as shown.

Robot A moved at 15 m/min. Robot B started 7 minutes after Robot A. Robot B took 14 minutes to reach the end point.

How many minutes later did Robot B reach the end point than Robot A? [3marks]
questions.answer: Time taken for Robot A = 240 ÷ 15 = 16 min | Time taken for Robot B = 7 + 14 = 21 min | 21 - 16 = 5 | Robot B reached the ending 5 min later than Robot A.

questions.transcribedStem: Ming worked from Monday to Friday in a job. The table shows the number of hours he worked on four out of the five days.
questions.answer: (a) (12 - 4) / 4 x 100% = 8 / 4 x 100% = 200% | The percentage increase in the amount Ming received was 200%. | (b) 4 units = 2 h | 12 units = 3 x 2 = 6 h | Ming worked 6 h on Wednesday. | (c) Number of units of money received on Thursday = 10 x 2 = 20 | Number of units of money received on Friday = 8 x 3 = 24 | 20 : 24 = 5 : 6 | The ratio of the amount Ming received on Thursday to that on Friday is 5 : 6.

questions.transcribedStem: Ishak packed some blue cards and 238 red cards in boxes. There were 7 cards in each box. In every 3 boxes that he packed, there was a total of 4 blue cards. How many boxes did Ishak use altogether to pack all the cards? [3marks]
questions.answer: 1 box -> 7 cards | 3 boxes -> 3 x 7 = 21 cards | Number of red cards in 3 boxes = 21 - 4 = 17 | Number of sets of 3 boxes = 238 ÷ 17 = 14 | 14 x 3 = 42 | Ishak used 42 boxes.

questions.transcribedStem:
In the figure, ABC, BFD and EFC are straight lines. BD = CD.
Find $\angle x$. [3marks]
questions.answer: ∠BCD = (180° - 62°) ÷ 2 (base angles of an isosceles triangle) = 59° | ∠BCF = 59° - 40° = 19° | ∠CBE = 180° - 86° (angles on a straight line) = 94° | ∠x = 180° - 19° - 94° (sum of angles in a triangle) = 67°

questions.transcribedStem:
The outline of the shaded figure is formed by 2 identical small quarter circles, 2 identical large quarter circles and 3 straight lines.

(Take $\pi$ = 3.14)
ABCD is a trapezium and ABGE is a rhombus. AFE, BFD and BEC are straight lines.
questions.answer: (a) ∠BGE = 180° - 58° - 58° (base angles of an isosceles triangle) = 64° | (b) ∠BAF = 64° (opposite angles of rhombus) | ∠DAF = 180° - 90° - 64° (angles between parallel lines, AB // DC) = 26° | (c) ∠ABF = 180° - 26° - 64° - 43° = 47° (sum of angles in a triangle) | ∠DBC = 180° - 47° - 58° - 64° (angles between parallel lines, AE // BG) = 11°

questions.transcribedStem: Meiyi had some books. She put $\frac{7}{9}$ of the books in 8 large boxes and $\frac{1}{12}$ of the books in 3 small boxes. She donated the rest of the books.
questions.answer: (a) 1 - 7/9 - 1/12 = 1 - 28/36 - 3/36 = 5/36 | 5/36 of the books were donated. | (b) 7/9 ÷ 8 = 7/9 x 1/8 = 7/72 | 7/72 of the books were put into each large box. | (c) 7/72 - (1/12 ÷ 3) = 7/72 - 1/36 = 5/72 | 5 units = 15 | 1 unit = 15 ÷ 5 = 3 | 72 x 3 = 216 | Meiyi had 216 books at first.

questions.transcribedStem:
Three rectangular tanks, P, Q and R, are shown.
The height of tank R is half the height of tank Q.

At first, tank P was filled with some water and tanks Q and R were empty.
Yong poured some water from tank P to fill tank Q completely.

The remaining water in tank P could only fill $\frac{3}{5}$ of tank R.

Find the height of tank R. [3marks]
questions.answer: Volume of water in P = 21 x 13 x (36 - 4) = 8736 cm³ | Total volume of Q and R in units = 12 x 10 x (10 + 3) units = 1560 | 1560 units = 8736 | 1 unit = 8736 ÷ 1560 = 5.6 | 5 units = 5 x 5.6 = 28 | The height of R is 28 cm.

questions.transcribedStem: ABC, DEF and PQR are three identical equilateral triangles.
questions.answer: (a) (41 + 3) - 18 = 26 | The length of AB is 26 cm. | (b) Base of the first overlapped triangle = 26 - 18 = 8 cm | 26 + 18 + 18 + 8 + 26 + 23 + 23 + 3 = 145 | The perimeter of the shaded parts is 145 cm.
